summary: A TU-95MS bomber departed Engels-2 for Ukrainian airfield Ukrainianka, while Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV attacks across several fronts, including a strike on the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant machine hall. Germany delivered an IRIS‑T launcher and Ukraine received another IRIS‑T system, amid reports of over 2,300 Russian drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles launched in one week. Russian UAV activity was also noted over the Black Sea and in the Dnipro region, and the Third Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk.
key_points: TU‑95MS bomber took off from Engels‑2 and is relocating to Ukrainianka airfield (Source: @eRadarrua/94528).
Ukrainian UAV group formed over the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/94529).
German IRIS‑T launcher donated to Ukraine, President Zelensky highlighted need for more missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30566).
Ukrainian drone struck the machine hall of Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, radiation levels reported normal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Russian drone attacked a target in Dnipro (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30567).
Ukraine reported over 2,300 Russian drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles launched in one week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Third Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes in Luhansk, striking enemy armor and ammunition depots up to 205 km deep (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).

sections.executive_summary: A TU‑95MS bomber departed Engels‑2 for Ukrainianka, indicating possible Russian air force redeployment. Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV activity across multiple fronts, including a strike on the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant machine hall. Germany’s IRIS‑T delivery and Ukraine’s claim of controlling logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk highlight escalating offensive and defensive operations.

points: Ukrainian forces reported a UAV group forming over the Black Sea, suggesting increased maritime drone operations (Source: @eRadarrua/94529).
A Ukrainian drone struck the machine hall of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, causing a through explosion but radiation levels remained normal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
The Third Army Corps announced control over logistics routes in Luhansk, with UAV pilots hitting enemy armor and ammunition depots up to 205 km inside occupied territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported in the Dnipro region, including groups heading toward Krivoy Rog and from the Black Sea toward Tatarbunary (Source: @kpszsu/63404).
Ukrainian sources noted that over 2,300 Russian drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles were launched against Ukrainian positions in a single week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
A TU‑95MS bomber took off from the Engels‑2 airbase around 21:00 and is currently relocating to the Ukrainianka aerodrome (Source: @eRadarrua/94528).
Russian drone activity was observed over the Dnipro area, with a specific attack on a target in Dnipro reported (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30567).
Russian forces continue to employ UAVs along the northern front, with reactive UAVs reported along the Belarus border in Chernihiv and northern Kyiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
Russian military maintains artillery and aviation superiority in Konstantinovka, using FPV drones for counter‑attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Russia’s Ministry of Health reported stable cholera situation domestically, unrelated to combat but indicating internal health monitoring (Source: @tass_agency/378331).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported in Sumy and Kharkiv regions, moving southward and westward, indicating pressure on Russian positions in the east (Source: @kpszsu/63432).
A UAV group from Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions moved toward the front, with several strike groups listed (Source: @eRadarrua/94531).
Russian forces in Konstantinovka are exploiting artillery and aviation superiority, razing the city and resisting Ukrainian advances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Ukrainian logistics and southern land corridor face constant drone attacks up to 200 km deep, hampering supply lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near the border of Sumy and Kharkiv heading toward Poltava direction (Source: @kpszsu/63407).
Reactive UAVs observed along the Belarus border in Chernihiv and northern Kyiv regions, heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
UAV activity reported near the northern border of Chernihiv, indicating surveillance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
Ukrainian UAVs detected on the border of Sumy and Kharkiv regions moving toward Poltava, suggesting north‑east pressure (Source: @kpszsu/63407).
Ukrainian UAVs reported in the north‑east of Kharkiv region with southern course, showing operational flexibility (Source: @kpszsu/63432).
No direct combat casualties reported on the northern front in the provided messages.
Ukrainian UAVs reported over the southern‑eastern outskirts of Dnipro with variable course (Source: @kpszsu/63418).
Ukrainian UAV strike on the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant machine hall occurred in the south‑eastern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Russian drone attacks on Dnipro resulted in a child fatality and six injuries in Henichesk (Source: @tass_agency/378336).
Ukrainian forces reported a Russian drone attack on a target in Dnipro (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30567).
Ukrainian logistics hub in Dnipro was hit, igniting a logistics company’s warehouse and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81569).
TU‑95MS bomber relocation from Engels‑2 to Ukrainianka suggests Russian strategic bomber movements (Source: @eRadarrua/94528).
Ukrainian UAVs are operating over the Black Sea, indicating maritime air‑space contest (Source: @eRadarrua/94529).
Russian UAV attacks continue in Dnipro and Henichesk, causing civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/378336, @ukr_leaks_eng/30567).
Ukraine reported over 2,300 Russian drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles launched in one week, reflecting intense aerial bombardment (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
German IRIS‑T launcher delivery enhances Ukrainian air defence capability against Russian aerial threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30566).

key_developments: A TU‑95MS bomber departed Engels‑2 and is relocating to Ukrainianka airfield, indicating possible Russian strategic bomber redeployment. (Source: @eRadarrua/94528)
Ukrainian UAVs formed a strike group over the Black Sea, expanding drone operations into maritime domains. (Source: @eRadarrua/94529)
A Ukrainian drone struck the machine hall of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, causing a through explosion but no radiation anomaly. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558)
Germany donated an IRIS‑T launcher to Ukraine, and Ukraine received an additional IRIS‑T system, strengthening air defence. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30566)
Russian forces launched over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles against Ukraine in a single week, reflecting intense aerial pressure. (Source: @UkraineNow/81568)
The Third Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes in occupied Luhansk, striking enemy armor and ammunition depots up to 205 km deep. (Source: @UkraineNow/81575)
A Russian drone attacked a target in Dnipro, adding to civilian casualties reported in Henichesk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30567)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial assets, including strategic bombers and high‑volume UAV attacks, continue to pressure Ukrainian defenses across multiple fronts. Ukraine’s expanding UAV operations, especially over the Black Sea and deep strikes into occupied territories, demonstrate growing offensive capabilities. The delivery of IRIS‑T systems enhances Ukrainian air defence but must contend with the sustained Russian drone and missile barrage.
sections.regional_response: Western partners, exemplified by Germany’s IRIS‑T donation, are bolstering Ukrainian air defence, while the United States is studying Ukrainian drone tactics for broader application. Russian diplomatic efforts focus on legal challenges in the UN, but these have limited immediate impact on battlefield dynamics. Local authorities in affected regions, such as Henichesk and Dnipro, are managing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV activity is highly dispersed, with groups operating from Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipro, and maritime zones, indicating a flexible command structure. Russian strategic bomber movements suggest possible redeployment or training activities at Ukrainianka. Continued monitoring of logistics route control in Luhansk is critical, as Ukrainian UAV strikes may disrupt Russian supply chains deep behind the front lines.
